findings.description: The 'index' header-position map is built but never used; rows are parsed by hardcoded positions, making it dead code.
Records are coerced by fixed positional indices (row[0..3]) instead of the header index, so any column reordering silently maps values to the wrong fields or crashes.
Short or empty rows (e.g. a trailing blank line) raise IndexError on row[0..3] because row length is never validated.
int(row[0]) and float(row[2]) raise an uncaught ValueError on any malformed cell, aborting the entire parse on a single bad row.
int(row[0]) rejects otherwise-valid numeric strings like '3.0' or ' 3', which may be too strict for real CSV input.
open(path) in count_rows is never closed (no context manager), leaking the file handle.
count_rows uses physical line count, so it miscounts rows containing quoted embedded newlines and disagrees with parse()'s real record count.
count_rows returns -1 for an empty file and counts a trailing blank line as a data row.
readlines() loads the entire file into memory, which is wasteful for large CSVs when only a count is needed.
list(reader) materializes the whole CSV in memory, so stream_valid does not actually stream parsing despite its name.
export trusts an attacker-influenced filename; basename blocks traversal but still allows overwriting arbitrary existing files within export_dir with no extension/whitelist check.
Constructor performs os.makedirs on a hardcoded /var/data/clean, causing a side effect and likely PermissionError at instantiation in many environments.
Monetary 'amount' is stored and summed as float, risking floating-point precision errors for financial data (Decimal would be safer).
summarize sums floats, accumulating rounding error before the round(2); borderline given amount is already float.
Email validation only checks for '@', accepting clearly invalid addresses; matches the stated contract but is very weak.
Missing-column check ignores duplicate headers; a repeated column name passes validation but the index map silently keeps only the last occurrence.
